source_notes.usage_note: Use IBCS standards and examples as directional reporting guidance only. Do not imply certification, endorsement, or affiliation.
source_notes.license_note: IBCS Standards are published under CC BY-SA 4.0; other IBCS website material can have separate restrictions. Keep source URLs and attribution with any cached asset.

source_notes.usage_note: Use UniformChart as a fixed visual reference source for common standardized chart and table families. Do not publish UniformChart examples inside our generated gallery.
source_notes.license_note: UniformChart provides free examples/templates and is inspired by IBCS but is not affiliated with the IBCS Institute or Association. Keep source URLs and attribution with any cached asset.

families.label: Waterfall and variance bridge
review_focus: Separate base, positive variance, negative variance, and final total with consistent visual semantics.
Keep variance notation compact and comparable across bars.
Avoid decorative color; use color only when it carries variance meaning.

families.label: Stacked bar and stacked column
review_focus: Use stacking only when segment composition is part of the message.
Keep total labels, segment labels, and legends from competing.
Suppress labels that do not add information, especially single-segment duplicates.

families.label: Column and bar
review_focus: Use clear measure, unit, and period in the title or subtitle.
Keep axis, labels, and value notation consistent across comparable charts.
Prefer restrained monochrome bars unless color encodes a real semantic difference.

families.label: Distribution
review_focus: Make the distribution shape readable before adding decorative detail.
Use units and sample scope so the reader knows what population is being compared.
Avoid dense annotations that obscure the statistical shape.

families.label: Line, area, and time-series
review_focus: Make the period grain, unit, and baseline explicit.
Use direct labels and sparse anchor labels instead of dense legends.
Keep comparable panels or series on consistent scales unless a different scale is explicitly disclosed.

families.label: Scatter and bubble
review_focus: State the x and y measures, units, and denominator clearly.
Use color or bubble size only when it encodes a meaningful dimension.
Keep labels sparse enough that outliers and clusters remain visible.

families.label: Mekko and mosaic
review_focus: Make both dimensions and their denominators explicit.
Keep labels legible inside variable-width cells.
Use sorting and grouping to make comparison possible without visual clutter.

families.label: Reporting table
review_focus: Use business rows and comparison columns rather than raw data dumps.
Align numbers, units, deltas, and percent deltas consistently.
Use thin separators, emphasized totals, and optional in-cell variance markers only when they improve scanability.

families.label: General reporting chart
review_focus: Start from the generated artifact and the user complaint.
Check title, unit, period, labeling, color semantics, clutter, and export mode.
Suggest adjacent issues separately from the requested change.
